TextView.android:text:
Setting weekly goals is a highly effective way to break down large ambitions into manageable steps, preventing overwhelm while maintaining a steady rhythm of progress.

 Core Strategies for Weekly Goal Setting

 1.Limit Your Focus: Aim for 3–5 key priorities per week. Setting too many goals can lead to burnout and diluted effort.

 2.Use the SMART Framework: Ensure every goal is Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound. For example, instead of exercise more, use run for 30 minutes three times this week.

 3.Reverse Engineer Large Goals: Start with your long-term objective (yearly or quarterly) and work backward to determine what needs to happen this week to stay on track.

 4.Time Blocking: Don't just list tasks; assign them specific slots on your calendar. Treat these as sacred appointments with yourself.

 5.The Top Goal Habit: Schedule your most important task for the first two hours of your morning when your energy and focus are typically at their highest.

 Essential Weekly Rituals

 i)The Monday Reset: Set your goals on Monday morning when optimism and clarity are highest. Use this time to brain dump all pending tasks before prioritizing the top three.

 ii)Mid-Week Checkpoints: Briefly review your progress on Wednesday or Thursday to identify blockers early and adjust before the week ends.

 iii)The Friday Analysis: Reflect on your wins and challenges. Ask yourself what worked well, what hindered you, and what you can improve for next week.
